title: Regal Rexnord Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividend of $.35 per share
author: Prnewswire
description: Regal Rexnord Corporation (NYSE:RRX) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.35 per share, payable on July 14, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 30, 2026. The company has maintained continuous quarterly dividend payments since January 1961. The Milwaukee-based industrial motion control company operates through three segments: Industrial Powertrain Solutions, Power Efficiency Solutions, and Automation & Motion Control.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a consistent quarterly dividend of $0.35 per share and has maintained uninterrupted dividend payments for over 65 years (since 1961), demonstrating financial stability and shareholder-friendly capital allocation. This indicates strong cash generation and management confidence in the business.

title: 3 Energy Stocks to Buy as AI Power Demand Surges—and 2 to Avoid
author: Bridget Bennett
description: As AI hyperscalers drive unprecedented electricity demand, the article identifies three energy infrastructure stocks positioned to benefit from the multi-year power buildout: MasTec (construction/engineering), Regal Rexnord (data center power management), and EQT (natural gas production). Two companies to avoid are CoreWeave (unprofitable data center operator) and Oklo (small modular reactor company with unfavorable economics).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong backlog of $19B providing years of revenue visibility, management guidance for 19% growth in 2026, and positioned as essential infrastructure builder for AI power buildout. Trading below intrinsic value relative to growth prospects.

Successfully pivoted to high-margin data center power management with E-Pod product. Q4 2025 orders of $735M and potential $1B revenue in data center business within two years. Stock volatility presents attractive entry point.

Largest U.S. natural gas producer with 9-12 years of reserves, lowest-cost production at $2/MMBtu, and dual catalysts from domestic power demand and LNG exports. Unhedged 2026 position provides upside on rising gas prices.

Described as 'WeWork of AI boom' with negative profitability (-22.74% profit margin), negative economic profit since IPO, and market pricing it at 5x typical data center operator returns. High debt ($29.8B) with weak liquidity (0.46 current ratio) and rising capex constraints.

Small modular reactor company behind competitors technologically. Leasing business model economics don't work at current pricing ($200-250/MWh cost vs. $400-500/MWh market pricing). Currently losing $100M annually while market prices in $400-500M earnings.

title: Software's Freefall Continues, Bitcoin Dips To 16-Month Lows: What's Moving Markets Thursday?
author: Piero Cingari
description: Software stocks plunged for an eighth consecutive session on Thursday, marking their longest losing streak since May 2021, as investors worry AI could undermine SaaS demand. Bitcoin fell 9% to $66,000, hitting 16-month lows, while commodities suffered broad declines. Major indices traded modestly lower, with notable losers including Estée Lauder (down 23% on tariff concerns), Palantir (down 4.4%), and Qualcomm (down 7% on weak guidance). Winners included McKesson (up 15.8%), Regal Rexnord (up 13%), and Uber (up 3%).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fell 1.9% and down roughly 30% from late-October highs; sector experiencing eighth consecutive losing session due to AI concerns impacting SaaS demand
Dropped 4.4% following an 11% slump on Wednesday; described as poster child of AI-driven software rally now facing significant headwinds

title: Regal Rexnord Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividend of $.35 per share
author: Prnewswire
description: Regal Rexnord Corporation (NYSE:RRX) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.35 per share, payable on April 14, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 31, 2026. The company has maintained continuous quarterly dividend payments since January 1961.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a consistent quarterly dividend of $0.35 per share and has maintained uninterrupted dividend payments for over 65 years (since 1961), demonstrating financial stability, profitability, and commitment to shareholder returns. title: Industrial Gearbox Market Size to Exceed USD 45.53 Billion by 2033 | Report by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The industrial gearbox market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 3.98-4.50% through 2033, driven by manufacturing automation, smart manufacturing trends, and renewable energy expansion. However, high manufacturing costs and complex installation requirements pose challenges, particularly for small-to-medium enterprises. Helical gearboxes dominate by type, while the manufacturing sector leads by end-user, with Asia Pacific commanding 36.58% market share.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Siemens introduced the SIMOGEAR Generation X.e series in March 2025, demonstrating innovation and market leadership in next-generation industrial gearbox technology with expanded torque ranges and modular designs.
ABB is listed as a leading market player but no specific recent developments or market activities are mentioned in the article.
Schaeffler is identified as a leading market player but no specific recent developments or market activities are mentioned in the article.
